title: ngrok authentication tokens should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: ngrok authentication token detected in source code. Auth tokens provide access to ngrok tunneling services and can expose internal applications to the internet. Exposing these tokens allows attackers to create unauthorized tunnels, intercept traffic, access internal development environments, and compromise sensitive data. Store tokens securely and rotate immediately if exposed.

ngrok authtoken detected in configuration file. Configuration-based auth tokens in ngrok.yml or similar config files provide persistent tunnel access. Exposing these tokens allows attackers to establish unauthorized tunnels to internal networks, intercept development traffic, and access private services. Use secure configuration management and environment variables instead.

ngrok authentication token variable detected in source code. Hardcoded ngrok tokens in variable assignments provide direct tunnel access credentials. Exposing these tokens enables attackers to create malicious tunnels, bypass firewalls, expose internal services, and intercept sensitive communications. Use environment variables or secure credential storage.

ngrok tunnel URL with embedded authentication detected. URLs containing authentication credentials expose tunnel access tokens in logs, history, and monitoring systems. This allows attackers to hijack active tunnels, intercept traffic, and access internal development environments. Never embed credentials in URLs; use proper authentication mechanisms.
